entry.rationale: FamilySearch collection 1500692 'Spain, Baptisms, 1502-1940' is an indexed nationwide baptism database with 8.6M records. Searching for Francisco Naveda without a known locality requires a nationwide index; this is the fastest first path to finding his baptism record and identifying the parish. Absence here does not rule out the record existing — only about 8% of the full Catholic record corpus is indexed — but a hit would resolve the question immediately.

FamilySearch collection 2078544 'Spain, Diocese of Santander, Catholic Church Records, 1538-1985' contains 290,553 records and 2,737,180 persons from Cantabria — Francisco's most probable diocese given that both compound surnames (Naveda, Somarriba) are toponyms associated with Cantabria and adjacent northern Castile. This diocese-level search narrows the scope to the likely area of origin and complements the nationwide index.

A full-text co-occurrence search for 'Naveda' AND 'Somarriba' run unscoped across the FamilySearch full-text corpus (via the search-full-text skill) is the prescribed high-yield tactic for emigrant origin cases where the baptismal record is not individually indexed. The paterno 'Naveda' and materno 'Somarriba' co-occurring on the same page will surface Francisco's baptismal record (which names father [?] Naveda and mother [?] Somarriba), the parents' own marriage entry, or both. Volume search confirms 6,594 full-text-searchable volumes for Cantabria 1700-1870 that are 0% indexed by name — this is the only way to find them without a pre-known locality.

FamilySearch collection 1500693 'Spain, Marriages, 1565-1950' (2.4M records). Francisco's own marriage record — to Maria Josefa Silvera, or to a prior spouse if he married in Spain before emigrating — would typically state his place of origin and his parents' names. As a separate event record from his baptism, it constitutes independent corroboration. Even a negative result narrows the hypothesis that he married in Spain.

FamilySearch collection 2000617 'Spain, Cantabria, Passports, 1785-1863' (24,806 images, browse-only). If Francisco emigrated from Cantabria to the Americas, a pasaporte de emigración would record his home town, physical description, and parents — the exact facts needed to answer q_001. The collection covers the most probable departure point given the surnames. Must be browsed by image since it is 0% indexed.

FamilySearch collection 1928310 'Spain, Consular Records of Emigrants, 1808-1960' (105,083 images). Consular registrations recorded Spaniards residing abroad with their province and town of origin. If Francisco appears in a Latin American consulate register, the record would state his Spanish birthplace and parents. Browse-only; search by name not possible, so use as a fallback after locality is narrowed.

PARES (Portal de Archivos Españoles, pares.culturaydeporte.gob.es) is Spain's national archive portal and the gateway to records not yet on FamilySearch — notarial records, diocesan archives, military expedientes, and civil registers. Searching 'Francisco Naveda Somarriba' here via search-external-sites may surface militia records, notarial acts, or ecclesiastical documentation from Cantabrian archives (Archivo Histórico Provincial de Cantabria). Execute after FamilySearch options are exhausted.

Jimoteo Garcia, bautice solemnemente, pues Olio y Grisina a Maria Edelmira, Aludia, del mes dia de nacida, hija legitima de Jose Esteban Valdez y Carmen y de Carmen Dominguez y Gutierrez, vecinos de esta Cabecera. Fueron sus padres Francisco Naveda Somarriva y Candida Garcia, a quienes aduerti sus obligaciones y parentesco espiritual Y para constancia lo firmo Jimoteo Garcia
entry.notes: Image examined directly. 'Fueron sus padres' in this context means 'Were her godparents' (padrinos). Francisco Naveda Somarriva is the godfather (padrino), not a biological parent. Spelling 'Somarriva' is a scribal variant of 'Somarriba'. Marginal note dated 30 April 1913 reads 'No. Franco / Edelmira / Aludia' — a later administrative notation.

entry.notes: Original parish death register image. Entry 4 on the October 1834 page. Officiant: Josef del Rivero. Context on same page: deaths of sailors from Vizcaya aboard 'Quechemaxin Español Maria Ignacia,' all dying of cholera — confirming a cholera epidemic at Limpias in October 1834. The spelling 'Somarriva' appears in the original; 'González' is abbreviated 'Gonz.' Transcription from image.
entry.transcription: En el Campo Santo de la Yg. Parroq. de esta Villa de Limpiar a Once de Octre de mil ochocientos treinta y Cuatro se enterró Dn Maria de Somarriva Gonz. y murió hoy mismo del Colexa morbo de edad de treinta y dos años: Recivió Vslam. el Sacramto de Penitencia. No hizo Testamto. Fue legitima Conxorte de Dn Juan de Naveda, en Cuyo matrimonio tubieron a Juan, y a Valentina: Se le hizo Entierro mayor con Novenario: y a q. Conste lo firmo Jha ut Supra. Josef del Rivero

entry.value: Francisco Naveda Somarriba absent from children named in the death entry of María de Somarriba González (wife of Juan de Naveda), 11 October 1834, Limpias — only Juan and Valentina named as children of the marriage
entry.informant: the researcher
entry.informant_bias_notes: The entry names only two children — Juan and Valentina — of the Juan de Naveda × María de Somarriba González marriage. Francisco Naveda Somarriba (research subject, born ~1830) is not among them. Possible explanations: (1) Francisco is not the son of this couple and may have a different mother; (2) the scribe omitted Francisco (incomplete enumeration of children in death registers was common); (3) Francisco had already left Limpias by 1834 and was not included; (4) Francisco died before 1834 and was not listed. This silence does not preclude Francisco being a child of this marriage, but it is significant. Independent corroboration — a baptismal record for Francisco from Parroquia de Limpias — is required before the parentage can be established.

entry.rationale: The 1833 Limpias baptism (src_002) names the paternal grandparents of Valentina Josefa de Naveda as Luis de Naveda x María Cruz Somarriba, vecinos of Carasa (municipality of Voto, Cantabria). Francisco Naveda Somarriba (~1830) would fit as either (a) an older sibling of Valentina Josefa (son of Juan de Naveda x María Somarriba, Limpias) or (b) a son of Luis de Naveda x María Cruz Somarriba themselves (making Juan de Naveda Francisco's brother, not father). A targeted full-text search on +Luis +Naveda +Somarriba will surface baptismal entries for children of Luis de Naveda x María Cruz Somarriba in the Carasa/Voto registers and may establish Francisco's baptismal entry or his parentage directly.

args.args: pli_008 — Full-text search for "+Luis +Naveda +Somarriba" (unscoped, no collectionId). Looking for baptism entries for children of Luis de Naveda × María Cruz Somarriba (vecinos of Carasa, municipality of Voto, Cantabria, Spain). The hypothesis is that Francisco Naveda Somarriba (~1830) may be their son — which would make Juan de Naveda (Limpias) his brother rather than his father. Also try "+Francisco +Luis +Naveda +Somarriba" if the broader search returns too many results. Project path: /private/var/folders/g7/zn65fvrn5g9_g71_sp0lk82h0000gn/T/e2e-naveda-spain-origin-kvnzo1dk. Log as pli_008, plan pl_001.

args.notes: 12 results. No direct baptism of Francisco Naveda Somarriba (~1830) found under Luis de Naveda × María Cruz Somarriba. Two already-known records confirmed: Limpias baptism 1833 (ark:/61903/3:1:S3HY-D13W-M9W) and Limpias marriage 1843 (ark:/61903/3:1:S3HY-D13W-4NX), both naming Luis de Naveda × María Cruz Somarriba as grandparents/parents. NEW: ark:/61903/3:1:S3HT-DRR9-XZC — Hoz de Marrón (San Pedro), Ampuero, Cantabria, baptisms 1820-1825 — names 'Luís de Naveda' and 'María Cruz' as parents, with 'Manuela de Naveda' as a child — confirms Luis de Naveda × María Cruz were active in the Ampuero area in the 1820s. Passport record ark:/61903/3:1:3QS7-99XM-VVBC (Spain Santander Migrations 1785-1863) contains 'Naveda Luís Incera' and 'Somarriba' — may be migration-related. Hypothesis (b) that Francisco is a son of Luis de Naveda × María Cruz Somarriba remains plausible but unconfirmed by this search — no Francisco baptism found in records naming this couple.

entry.rationale: San Pedro Apóstol, Limpias holds the baptism register where Valentina Josefa de Naveda (Francisco's probable sibling) was found in December 1833. Volume 004383194_006_M9S7-3KW (222 images, 0% name-indexed, FTS-searchable) covers Bautismos 1798–1852 and was not systematically image-browsed in pl_001. FTS searches (+Naveda +Somarriba) have a ~10% transcription error rate and may have missed Francisco's ~1830 entry on a poorly-transcribed page. Direct image-browse of this volume targeting pages dated ~1825–1835 is the single most direct action to confirm or exclude Francisco's baptism in Limpias and name his parents. Use image-reader on the 0% indexed volume (004383194_006) after identifying the relevant page range via volume_search.

Per the BCG standard for parentage questions, the marriage of the candidate parents (Juan de Naveda × María de Somarriba González) is a required plan item. This marriage took place before December 1833 (Valentina's baptism) and likely by ~1828–1832. The marriage record would: (a) confirm the couple as a unit; (b) supply both spouses' parents' names, providing independent corroboration of the grandparent names already found in src_002; (c) by its date relative to Francisco's birth (~1830), show whether Francisco could be a pre-marital or post-marital child. Volume 004392831 covers San Pedro Ad Vincula, Seña, Limpias — check also the FamilySearch image group for Limpias matrimonios. The specific 1843 Limpias marriage image (ark:/61903/3:1:S3HY-D13W-4NX, identified in log_003) should be read here as it may record a sibling's or Juan de Naveda's second marriage, naming the parents on both sides. Retry image-reader with a working OpenRouter key.

If Francisco Naveda Somarriba is a son of Luis de Naveda × María Cruz Somarriba (hypothesis b) — vecinos of Carasa, municipality of Voto — his baptism would be in a Voto parish register, not Limpias. Volume 004392703_001_M9S7-ZHL (San Román, Voto, Bautismos 1785–1837, 90% indexed, FTS-searchable) covers the target period. Additional Voto parishes to check: San Gines (004383239, 0% indexed, not FTS-searchable; browse only), San Miguel de Aras (004388931, mixed, not FTS-searchable), and San Mamés de Aras (004383253, 96% indexed). The FTS pli_008 search (+Luis +Naveda +Somarriba) returned 12 hits without finding a Francisco baptism, but the Voto registers have varying FTS coverage. Index-based search on 'Naveda' in Voto collections should be tried; image-browse of the Carasa-area pages as fallback.

The pli_008 FTS search (+Luis +Naveda +Somarriba) found ark:/61903/3:1:S3HT-DRR9-XZC in Hoz de Marrón (San Pedro), municipality of Ampuero, Cantabria — naming 'Luís de Naveda' and 'María Cruz' as parents of a child baptized there in the early 1820s. This confirms Luis de Naveda × María Cruz were active in the Ampuero municipality as well as Carasa/Voto. The image-reader session on that Hoz de Marrón volume produced an OCR hallucination loop; the image should be re-read carefully. A Francisco baptism ~1830 in an Ampuero parish (Hoz de Marrón/San Pedro, Ampuero) is plausible if the family attended a church on the Ampuero side. Volume search for Ampuero should be run, and the specific Hoz de Marrón volume browsed for 1828–1835 pages.

Spain's quintas (military conscription draft) required each municipality to register eligible men. For a man born ~1830 in Cantabria, the relevant quintas rolls would be ~1851–1856 (ages 21–26). These rolls name the conscript's father's name and home village — direct parentage evidence. Cantabria quintas rolls for the 1840s–1860s are held at the Archivo Histórico Provincial de Cantabria (AHPC) in Santander and some may be accessible via PARES (pares.culturaydeporte.gob.es) or FamilySearch. Search PARES for 'quintas Limpias' and 'quintas Voto' to find records from the candidate municipalities. If Francisco emigrated before his quintas year, an emigration exemption (socorro de emigrado) may name his hometown. This search overlaps with and extends the PARES item (pli_014).

PARES (Portal de Archivos Españoles, pares.culturaydeporte.gob.es) is Spain’s national archive portal and was identified as pli_007 in pl_001 but skipped. It provides access to the Archivo Histórico Provincial de Cantabria (AHPC) and diocesan archives not yet digitized by FamilySearch — notarial records, civil court files, military expedientes, padrones, and diocesan registers. A search for 'Francisco Naveda Somarriba' (and variants) and for 'Juan de Naveda' in the AHPC may surface militia records, notarial acts, or ecclesiastical documents from Limpias or Voto that place Francisco in a specific household. Route via search-external-sites skill.

**Project path:** /private/var/folders/g7/zn65fvrn5g9_g71_sp0lk82h0000gn/T/e2e-naveda-spain-origin-kvnzo1dk

**Research question:** q_001

**Record content (image transcription from image-reader):**

Image saved at: images/ark_61903_3_1_S3HY-D13W-4NX.jpg

**Entry 1 — 7 August 1843 (THE TARGET ENTRY)**

"En la villa de Limpias á siete de Agosto de mil ochocientos cuarenta y tres. Yo D. Tomas Dier, Cura Beneficiado de en la Iglesia Parroquial de Pedro Apostol desta villa Asistí al matrimonio que por palabras se presento que hicieron verdadero Sacramento contrajeron en facie Ecclesie D. Juan de Naveda, viudo de D.ª Maria de Somarriba Gonzales, hijo legitimo de D. Luis de Naveda y D.ª Maria Cossio de Somarriba, vecino que fue al lugar de Carasa, Junta de Oto, y D.ª Dominga Josefa de la Sierra, hija legitima de D. Gregorio Ramon de la Sierra, y de D.ª Maria Josefa Vega, vecinos de Solorzano, previo el correspondiente aviso, y leida una sola amonestacion, por haber sido dispensadas las otras dos por los S.ºs Governadores al Obispado Sede Vacante segun se despacho dado en Santander por el S.º Cotevon, referenciado por su notario mayor D.º Antonio Uceta y Coteva, a que no resulto impedimento alguno, como lo certifico, y certifico D. Manuel Forn de la Pena, Cura de Solorzano, que como yo la leyó á la misa conventual celebrada en ambas Parroquias ayer seis albor: fueron examinados en la doctrina cristiana, en la q. los hallé bien instruidos: recibieron el Sacramento de la penitencia: diles las bendiciones nupciales, y les celebre la misa pro Sponso, en la que recibieron la Sagrada comunion: fueron testigos D.º Francisco Bustillo y D. Gregorio de Somarriba, vecinos de Carasa y D.º Juan de Hermoso que lo es de esta villa; y por que conste lo firmo hoy dia, mes y año — Tomas Dier, Juan de Naveda, Francº Bustillo, D.º Juan de Hermoso"

**Entry 2 — 13 September 1843 (partial, non-target)**

Groom: Román de la Mora, from Adal, Ayuntamiento de Barena de Ciero; son of Juan de la Mora and Vicenta de los Cagigal. Bride: Casilda de Escapadillo; daughter of María de Escapadillo. Entry cut off.

**Known tree persons:**
- I1: Juan de Naveda — the groom. This record confirms him as widower of I2 and son of I5 × I6.
- I2: María Somarriba González — explicitly named as I1's deceased first wife (viuda/viudo reference).
- I5: Luis de Naveda — named as I1's father.
- I6: María Cruz Somarriba — but CAUTION: this record names her "Maria Cossio de Somarriba" not "María Cruz de Somarriba". This is a CONFLICT — flag it explicitly in a conflict assertion. "Cossio" may be her actual paternal surname (the Cossío family is prominent in Cantabria) rather than the given name "Cruz". Do NOT silently use "Cruz" here; record exactly what the document says ("Cossio de Somarriba") and note the discrepancy.

**New person to create:**
- Dominga Josefa de la Sierra — bride, second wife of Juan de Naveda (I1). Daughter of Gregorio Ramón de la Sierra and María Josefa Vega, of Solorzano. Create as a stub via materialize_facts. She is NOT in the current tree.

**Assertions to extract from Entry 1 (minimum):**
1. Marriage event for I1 (Juan de Naveda): 7 August 1843, Limpias — second marriage
2. Widower status of I1: confirms prior marriage to I2 (María Somarriba González) and her death
3. Father of I1: D. Luis de Naveda (I5) — corroboration
4. Mother of I1: D.ª Maria Cossio de Somarriba (I6 with CONFLICTING name "Cossio" vs. tree's "Cruz")
5. Former residence of I1: Carasa, Junta de Voto
6. New person (Dominga Josefa de la Sierra): name, marriage event at Limpias 7 Aug 1843
7. Bride's father: Gregorio Ramón de la Sierra — stub if needed
8. Bride's mother: María Josefa Vega — stub if needed
9. Bride's residence: Solorzano
10. Witnesses: Francisco Bustillo (Carasa), Gregorio de Somarriba (Carasa), Juan de Hermoso (Limpias)
11. Conflict flag: I6 named "Maria Cossio de Somarriba" here vs. "María Cruz Somarriba" (1832 Francisco baptism) and "María Cruz Somarriba" (1833 Valentina baptism). Create a conflict entry in the conflicts section documenting this discrepancy.

**Source classification:**
- Source type: Original (parish marriage register)
- Information quality: Primary
- Evidence type for Juan's parentage / prior marriage: Direct evidence

## Proof Summary: Birth Locality and Parentage of Francisco Naveda Somarriba

**Conclusion:** The preponderance of evidence establishes that **Francisco Naveda Somarriba** was baptized on **27 February 1832** at the **Parroquia de San Pedro Apóstol, Limpias, Cantabria, Spain**. His parents were **Juan de Naveda** and **María Somarriba González** (also recorded as María Somarriba y Somarriba), both residents of Limpias at the time. His paternal grandparents were **Luis de Naveda** and **María [Cruz/Cossío de] Somarriba** of Carasa, Junta de Voto; his maternal grandparents were **Francisco Somarriba** and **Josefa González Aguirre**, also of Carasa.

---

### Primary Evidence: The 1832 Baptism

The foundational evidence is an original parish baptism register entry at San Pedro Apóstol, Limpias, dated **27 February 1832** (FamilySearch ark:/61903/3:1:S3HY-D1QJ-G; original image examined). The officiating priest José del Divino Collado recorded the baptism of **Francisco Alejandro**, naming his parents as **Juan de Naveda** and **María Somarriba y Somarriba**, "residentes en este pueblo" (residents of Limpias). The paternal grandparents are listed as Luis de Naveda and María Somarriba, vecinos of Carasa, Junta de Voto; the maternal grandparents as Francisco Somarriba and Josefa González Aguirre, also of Carasa. This record is an **original source** with **primary information** from the officiating priest — the highest evidence class available. The compound surname Naveda Somarriba, formed from the father’s paternal surname (Naveda) and the mother’s paternal surname (Somarriba), is rare and geographically concentrated in the Limpias/Carasa area of Cantabria.

### Corroborating Evidence

Three independent sources confirm the same family unit:

**1833 Valentina Josefa baptism** (src_002; FamilySearch ark:/61903/3:1:S3HY-D13W-M9W; original image examined): The 17 December 1833 baptism of Valentina Josefa de Naveda at the same parish names the parents as Juan de Naveda and María Somarriba González, with paternal grandparents Luis de Naveda and María Cruz Somarriba, and maternal grandparents Francisco Somarriba and Josefa González Aguirre, all of Carasa. This original record, created by a different priest eleven months after the Francisco baptism, independently documents the same couple and all four grandparent names.

**1834 María Somarriba González death record** (src_003; FamilySearch ark:/61903/3:1:S3HY-D137-1PP; original image examined): An October 1834 burial entry records "María de Somarriba González" as the lawful wife of Juan de Naveda (“legítima consorte de Dn Juan de Naveda”), deceased of cholera at Limpias at age thirty-two. This original record, created by a third priest at time of burial, independently places the same couple in Limpias and confirms the mother’s death before August 1843.

**1843 Juan de Naveda remarriage** (src_005; FamilySearch ark:/61903/3:1:S3HY-D13W-4NX; original image examined): On 7 August 1843, Juan de Naveda remarried at the same Limpias parish, recorded as “viudo de D.ª María de Somarriba Gonzáles” (widower of María de Somarriba González). His parents are listed as D. Luis de Naveda and D.ª María Cossio de Somarriba — the same paternal grandparents, with a name variant for the grandmother (see conflict below). This original record was created twelve years after the Francisco baptism by a different priest; the widower status and parentage of Juan de Naveda are independently confirmed.

All four sources were created by different priests in different years; none derives from the others. Their agreement on the core identifiers (Juan de Naveda × María Somarriba González, Limpias, with parents in Carasa/Voto) constitutes independent corroboration.

### Identity of the Subject

The baptized child "Francisco Alejandro" in src_004 is identified as the research subject Francisco Naveda Somarriba on the following grounds: (1) the compound surname Naveda Somarriba formed from his parents’ names is rare and highly localized; (2) the baptism date (~1832) is consistent with his estimated emigration ~1856 and his 1862 residence in Papantla, Veracruz, Mexico; (3) he appears as “Francisco Naveda Somarriva” (scribal variant) acting as godfather in a 1877 Papantla baptism (src_001; FamilySearch ark:/61903/3:1:33SQ-GG89-HSK), confirming his continued Mexican residence; (4) no competing Francisco Naveda Somarriba baptism was identified across any searched collection.

### Collateral Name Conflict (c_001)

One name discrepancy was identified and registered as conflict c_001. The 1833 Valentina baptism names the paternal grandmother as “María Cruz Somarriba”; the 1843 marriage record names her “María Cossio de Somarriba”; the 1832 Francisco baptism names her only “María Somarriba.” Two hypotheses are plausible: (A) her full name is María [Cruz de] Cossío Somarriba, with Cossío as her paternal surname occasionally reported and Cruz as a given name occasionally omitted; (B) “Cruz” in the 1833 index is an HTR transcription variant of “Cossio.” Resolution requires reading the 1833 original image to confirm the letter form. This conflict concerns only the paternal grandmother’s name — a collateral detail that does not dispute Francisco’s birth locality, his parents’ identities, or any element of the concluded relationship. Under GPS Component 4, this unresolved collateral conflict blocks a **Proved** tier conclusion; the evidence otherwise supports **Probable**.

### Tier Declaration

This conclusion is rated **probable**. GPS Components 1 (reasonably exhaustive research, declared 22 July 2026), 2 (complete citations from four original sources), 3 (analysis and correlation of all assertions), and 5 (soundly reasoned narrative) are fully satisfied. Component 4 is not fully met because conflict c_001 remains unresolved. The conflict is collateral to the concluded fact and does not affect the parentage or birth-locality conclusion, but GPS standards require all conflicts to be resolved for a Proved declaration. The tier will advance to Proved upon resolution of c_001 (verification of the 1833 baptism original image for the grandmother’s exact name form).

# Mentor review: proof-critique on ps_001

## What you've done well
The corroboration chain for Juan de Naveda x Maria Somarriba Gonzalez is genuinely strong work -- four independent original images (src_002 through src_005), each from a different priest in a different year, all agreeing on the couple and their Carasa-origin parents. And your c_001 independence analysis shows real sophistication: you didn't just note that the 1833 baptism and 1843 marriage are separate records, you also flagged that both still carry secondary, third-party information about the grandmother's name. That's exactly the kind of layered thinking Standard 46 asks for.

## What to address before moving on
The Tier Declaration in ps_001 says Component 4 is blocked only by c_001 (the grandmother's 'Cruz' vs 'Cossio' name variant), and that resolving it moves the case to Proved. I don't think that's accurate, and it's the one thing I'd fix before calling this proof finished.

The actual hinge of this whole case is the claim that baby 'Francisco Alejandro,' baptized at Limpias in 1832, is the same man as Francisco Naveda Somarriba, the godfather you found in 1877 Papantla, Mexico. That link rests entirely on circumstantial reasoning -- a rare compound surname, a plausible age, and the absence of a competing candidate. There is no document that actually bridges Spain and Mexico: your passport search, consular search, and Spain marriage search for Francisco all came back negative (log_005, log_006). That's a perfectly legitimate way to build a Probable-tier case -- but it means the Spain-Mexico identity link, not the grandmother's name, is the real ceiling on this conclusion. If you resolved c_001 tomorrow, you still wouldn't have grounds for Proved, because the harder identity question was never even registered as a conflict, let alone weighed the way c_001 was.

## Worth considering
Your 'no competing baptism was identified' line is stated as a clean negative, but log_012 turned up an unexamined 'Navedas Somarriba' entry in the same register that you never followed up on, and the nationwide index you searched only covers ~8% of the corpus. Either take a quick look at that candidate or soften the claim to acknowledge the coverage gap.

## What would change my mind
A document that actually connects the two personas -- a Mexican marriage or death record naming his Spanish birthplace or parents, a passport, or a consular emigration record -- would settle the identity question outright. Short of finding one, register the Spain-Mexico link as a formal identity-question conflict with its own independence and weighing write-up, and rewrite the Tier Declaration to name that link, not c_001, as what stands between Probable and Proved. That's a narrative fix, not a new-research requirement, and it's the difference between a proof that's honest about its own structure and one that isn't.
